plan_analysis:
Forecasters will mainly need three kinds of information that are still missing:

1. Historical base-rate data: month-by-month Southwest Land Border Encounter totals going back at least 10 years so they can build seasonal baselines, growth rates, and forecast models. They will also want to know how often March has deviated sharply from February, and by how much.

2. Resolution details and timing: confirmation of how CBP labels months, how quickly it publishes preliminary numbers, and whether later revisions are material. This can be learned by examining prior CBP data releases.

3. Current trajectory and potential shocks: the most recent (Jan–Feb 2026) encounter numbers plus any policy, diplomatic, or crisis developments that could cause March 2026 to spike or drop (e.g., new executive actions, agreements with Mexico, weather disasters in origin countries). A contrarian search looks for evidence of a coming collapse in flows—e.g., mass deportations, transit-country crack-downs—that would make an unexpectedly low number plausible.

The search strategy therefore allocates five HISTORICAL queries to pull structured time-series data and release-timing patterns, and three CURRENT queries to capture the latest CBP figures and news about policy or situational changes affecting March 2026 flows.

queries_planned.query: cbp “Southwest Land Border Encounters” monthly dataset
queries_planned.intent: Locate an official or mirrored CSV/Excel file with the full month-by-month encounter history for quick download and analysis.
Find charts or tables that summarise the last decade of monthly data to gauge long-run seasonality and variance.

queries_planned.query: cbp release schedule past months encounters
queries_planned.intent: Determine when CBP typically posts each month’s figures and whether preliminary numbers are later revised, clarifying the resolution mechanism.
Pull the specific March values for at least 2014-2025 to quantify a March-specific baseline.

queries_planned.query: Provide a table of U.S. CBP Southwest Land Border Encounters by month from FY2010 through Feb 2026, note any revisions, and compute average m/m change from Feb to Mar.
queries_planned.intent: Produce a clean historical series plus descriptive statistics the forecaster can plug straight into a model.
Retrieve the most recently released official number (or credible leak) for February 2026 to anchor near-term trend projections.
Surface breaking reports of unusual increases that might foreshadow a higher March figure.

queries_planned.query: new U.S. border policy 2026 crackdown Mexico transit
queries_planned.intent: Look for coverage of fresh enforcement agreements or policy changes that could sharply reduce March encounters, providing a contrarian scenario.
